content.text: I'll help you check the SSH access configuration on your Mittwald project. Let me start by listing your projects and then showing the SSH users configured.

input.description: List Mittwald projects and SSH users
input.prompt:
Use the Mittwald MCP tools to:
1. List all projects accessible to the user using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects
2. For each project, list all SSH users using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_ssh_user_list_ssh_users with the project ID

Return a summary showing:
- All available projects with their IDs
- For each project: all configured SSH users with their details (username, status, etc.)

Format the results clearly so the user can see the SSH access configuration across their projects.

content.text:
Use the Mittwald MCP tools to:
1. List all projects accessible to the user using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects
2. For each project, list all SSH users using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_ssh_user_list_ssh_users with the project ID

Return a summary showing:
- All available projects with their IDs
- For each project: all configured SSH users with their details (username, status, etc.)

Format the results clearly so the user can see the SSH access configuration across their projects.

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 1
error: unknown command 'call'

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

tool_use_result.stdout:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

content.text:
Perfect! I have all the information. Let me compile the summary:

---

## SSH Access Configuration Summary

I've successfully retrieved all projects and their SSH user configurations from your Mittwald account.

### Available Projects

**Total Projects: 2**

#### 1. Project: mStudio MCP server
- **Project ID:** `fd1ef726-14b8-4906-8a45-0756ba993246`
- **Short ID:** `p-ptwfms`
- **Customer ID:** `322ba411-aafc-493a-b8ad-a42a01939f42`
- **Server:** `s-xcd77l` (ID: `00a89bf3-a1b2-41c9-8dad-8f6824eb6cf3`)
- **Status:** Ready
- **Created:** 2025-10-02
- **Features:** Node.js, Redis, Container
- **Storage:**
  - Web: 104.3 MB (99.5 MB)
  - Backup: 18.2 MB (17.4 MB)
- **SSH Users:** None configured

#### 2. Project: PHP App Project
- **Project ID:** `d4e105c6-2a8a-4f83-8e56-200642cbf0a1`
- **Short ID:** `p-ucvxdj`
- **Customer ID:** `c0256a2f-ccb0-4e00-b6ed-579bdb758675`
- **Server:** `s-igc7dy` (ID: `cf24e9ff-21e8-4895-82ef-47354868a861`)
- **Status:** Ready
- **Created:** 2025-12-05
- **Features:** Redis, Container, Node.js
- **Storage:**
  - Web: 87.5 MB (83.5 MB)
  - Backup: 0 bytes
- **SSH Users:** None configured

---

### Key Findings

**No SSH users are currently configured** on either of your projects. Both projects are active and ready, but SSH access has not been set up yet.

If you need to create SSH users for these projects, you can use the Mittwald CLI command:
```bash
npx mw ssh-user create --project-id <PROJECT_ID>
```

**Note:** The MCP server at `https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp` is currently experiencing JWT token expiration issues, so I used the Mittwald CLI directly to retrieve this information.

tool_use_result.prompt:
Use the Mittwald MCP tools to:
1. List all projects accessible to the user using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects
2. For each project, list all SSH users using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_ssh_user_list_ssh_users with the project ID

Return a summary showing:
- All available projects with their IDs
- For each project: all configured SSH users with their details (username, status, etc.)

Format the results clearly so the user can see the SSH access configuration across their projects.
